scores.correctness_notes: The response teaches how to *write* a charm library from scratch rather than how to *use* existing charmlibs (find the interface library listing, add PyPI package or fetch-lib, implement the provider contract). This is a meaningful mismatch with what the gold standard describes. The mechanics described are largely correct, but the core premise is off.
scores.hallucination_notes: event.relation.save(data, self._charm.app) — the ops Relation object has no .save() method; this is fabricated. Standard ops writes to relation data via relation.data[app]['key'] = 'value'. The rest of the ops APIs (EventBase, ObjectEvents, EventSource, Object, observe, etc.) are real.
